scope_note: QA Unequal-k CCR Invariant Cert family [262]: backfills MC-1 and MC-2 of docs/theory/QA_QFT_ETCR_CROSSMAP.md §4.1 with an explicit QA-native unequal-k propagator. Source grounding: Mannheim, P.D., 'Equivalence of light-front quantization and instant-time quantization,' Phys. Rev. D 102 025020 (2020), arXiv:1909.03548, eqs (2.2) + (8.9)/(8.16) — unequal-time commutator as c-number invariant, Lehmann spectral representation. Companion to cert [260] QA Orbit-Dirac Bracket which delivers MC-3/MC-4 (Blaschke-Gieres arXiv:2004.14406). Cross-map paper section papers/in-progress/qft-etcr-orbit-quotient/section.md §3 and §6. Construction: i_Delta_QA(Delta_k; (b,e), (b',e')) := 1 if T^|Delta_k|(b,e) = (b',e') else 0 — the integer-valued T-orbit trajectory indicator. Equal-k limit (Delta_k = 0) recovers the stipulated delta-function CCR [a_{b,e}, a_dag_{b',e'}] = delta_{b,b'} delta_{e,e'}. Orbit decomposition: propagator vanishes when endpoints are in different T-orbits. Periodicity: propagator has period equal to the orbit period. Lehmann-type trace decomposition: Tr i_Delta_QA(Delta_k) = sum over orbit classes O of |O| * 1[period(O) divides Delta_k]. For m=9 under T_F: Tr i_Delta_QA(Delta_k) = 72*1[24 divides Delta_k] + 8*1[8 divides Delta_k] + 1. Verified exhaustively at Delta_k in {0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, 16, 24}. Stipulated CCR encoding is observer-side and not derived from QA primitives — consistent with precision guardrail §4.1 of cross-map. Scope v1: m=9, T_F dynamics only; m=24 deferred. Checks UKC_1+EQ_LIMIT/ORBIT_DECOMP/PERIODICITY/LEHMANN/A1/SRC/WITNESS/F; 1 PASS + 1 FAIL; self-test ok. Will Dale + Claude 2026-04-21.
